entries.description: Confirmed prerequisites at session start: Set 045 closed 2026-05-26 (PyPI 0.8.0 + Marketplace 0.21.0), Set 036 closed 2026-05-24. Operator locked $5 NTE budget for Set 046 and confirmed Session 1 scope = audit pass with two-pass devil's-advocate cross-provider consensus per feedback_devils_advocate_default_for_roadmap_decisions. Operator added three locked deliverables: (a) 0/? fraction for stub-mode sets, (b) second-line engine/model/effort badge on in-progress rows (suppress silently), (c) README screenshot with one in-progress half-done, two not-started, two completed.

Invoked python -m ai_router.start_session with engine=claude, model=claude-opus-4-7, effort=high, provider=anthropic. State-file flipped from not-started to in-progress with the orchestrator block populated; sessions[] gained a single in-progress placeholder; totalSessions defaulted to 1 by writer (later corrected to 7 in Step 9). Observed in-flight: this writer default for totalSessions is exactly the behavior deliverable (a) requires fixing — flagged for the audit and now in S2 scope.

Delegated grounding research to an Explore subagent to keep the main-context budget for the audit synthesis. Subagent located: (1) fractionFor() at providers/CustomSessionSetsView.ts:142-147 already returns N/? when totalSessions == null (Set 036 S7 work), so deliverable (a) is a writer-side fix not an Explorer fix; (2) (needs migration) detector at fileSystem.ts:373-381 fires on schemaVersion-absent OR schemaVersion-3-with-missing-sessions[]; today's right-click migrate action handles only the former; (3) in-progress row template at media/session-sets-tree/client.js:241-279 has a clean insertion point between .row-name and .harvest-badges inside .row-text for deliverable (b)'s second line, no DOM collision with badges/pills.

Authored docs/proposals/2026-05-26-explorer-enrichment-from-harvest-records/proposal.md (~400 lines, ~20K chars): code-state grounding (§2), candidate dispositions (§3 — 6 leverage points classified IN SCOPE / DEFER / ALREADY-SHIPPED), parked architectural question handling (§4 — v4 schema deferred to its own audit per feedback_audit_then_spec_for_substantial_features), README screenshot scoping (§5), 6-session breakdown (§6), 5 bias-cautions for the verifier (§7), 4 open questions for the verifier (§8), 7 memory-hook citations (§9). Self-authored per the in-session router restriction (feedback_ai_router_usage); cross-provider verification routed at the audit's own end-of-session boundary.

Ran Pass A of two-pass devil's-advocate consensus via run_consensus.py driver. Primary read (route, gemini-2.5-pro tier 2): ENDORSE WITH REVISIONS — endorsed §3 dispositions and §4 parked-question handling; recommended splitting Session 5 (migrator + UI) into two sessions for the 7-session arc; flagged Pass A primary's own missing leverage point (state-divergence pill — though the verify pass correctly noted this was already in §7 Bias 1). Cross-provider verify (gpt-5-4-mini): ISSUES FOUND — §3.3/§3.4 should be re-labeled ALREADY SHIPPED not DEFER (terminology fix); Pass A primary's 7-session split was a false-positive on Bias 4 specifically (justified, but verifier wanted stronger evidence); Q1 retroactive-migration question went unaddressed in Pass A primary. Pass A cost: $0.0487.

Ran Pass B (devil's-advocate framing) of consensus. Primary read (gemini-2.5-pro): ENDORSE WITH SPECIFIC BIAS FLIPS — flipped Bias 1 (harvested-primary for second-line), Bias 2 (summed cost), Bias 4 (split migrator), Bias 5 (move README earlier); stood by Bias 3. Open questions: Q1 forward-only no migration, Q2 .row-text placement, Q3 current copy-paste OK, Q4 static PNG. Cross-provider verify (gpt-5-4-mini): ISSUES FOUND — pushed back on Bias 1 flip (proposal's canonical-primary matches operator-locked wording 'the checked-out orchestrator' better than the inversion) and Bias 2 flip (router-primary preserves transparency better than summing). The verify-pushback on Pass B was the decisive vote on Biases 1 and 2 — net STAND BY proposal on both. Pass B cost: $0.0430.

Authored docs/proposals/2026-05-26-explorer-enrichment-from-harvest-records/verdict.md synthesizing the 4-way consensus into a bias-resolution table. Final dispositions: Bias 1 STAND BY (3-1), Bias 2 STAND BY (3-1), Bias 3 STAND BY with terminology fix (DEFER → ALREADY SHIPPED), Bias 4 FLIP (migrator gets own session — 6→7-session arc), Bias 5 partial flip (README stays in S7 close-out, fixture work surfaced as risk). Open questions all per Pass B primary (uncontested by verifier): Q1 forward-only, Q2 .row-text, Q3 current pattern + checklist, Q4 static PNG. New scope item from Pass A primary §5: state-divergence pill added to S3 alongside deliverable (b).

Replaced the stub spec.md at docs/session-sets/046-explorer-enrichment-from-harvest-records/spec.md with the audit-locked 7-session spec. New sections: §1 session-by-session breakdown with scope + layer per session, §2 operator-locked deliverables (a)/(b)/(c) map to sessions, §3 already-shipped expansion deferrals (writer-bypass pill + engine-mismatch pill from 0.21.0), §4 deferred sets (047 v4 schema audit, tool-touch histogram, §3.3/§3.4 expansions), §5 data-source rules (canonical-primary / harvested-fallback / suppress-silently for second-line, cost, idle-time), §6 non-goals, §7 risk register, §8 cumulative spend tracking.

Updated session-state.json sessions[] to reflect the audit-locked 7-session arc: added entries 2-7 with their titles from spec.md §1, bumped totalSessions from 1 (writer default) to 7. Sessions 2-7 status: not-started. Session 1 remains in-progress until close_session flips it. Note: this manual update is a workaround for the writer's totalSessions default — the proper fix ships in S2.

Opened the new audit-pending stub at docs/session-sets/047-state-file-schema-v4-audit/spec.md. The 047 stub bundles the v4 schema migration (derive top-level state from sessions[]) AND the blocked-on-prereqs lifecycle question parked at Set 044/S6, per feedback_audit_then_spec_for_substantial_features (substantial schema migration wants its own audit set followed by a separate implementation set). Pre-audit material lifted from the original 046 stub spec; cross-provider audit topics listed for the 047 S1 audit pass to address.

Wrote three new project memories: (1) project_set_046_session_1_audit_locked — captures the 7-session arc + canonical-primary rule + audit cost $0.183; (2) project_set_047_v4_schema_audit_stub — captures the 047 stub creation and prerequisite chain; (3) project_session_state_auto_creation_observed — captures an in-flight observation that writing only spec.md to a new session-sets subdirectory appeared to auto-create a non-canonical session-state.json (missing sessions[]/completedSessions[] arrays), suggesting an extension auto-create path that emits the same non-canonical shape needs-migration detector fires on. Updated existing memory project_needs_migration_lightweight_repo_observation to note Set 046 S6 absorbs the triage. Updated MEMORY.md index.

Authored close-out artifacts: disposition.json (status=completed, verification_method=manual, next_orchestrator=claude continue-current-trajectory for S2), .close-reason-s1.md (attestation that the two-pass cross-provider audit IS the verification for this session — re-routing through close_session's verifier queue would duplicate the work at additional router cost with no marginal signal), this activity-log.json. Operator opted for --manual-verify based on the audit-session character.

Invoked python -m ai_router.start_session for session 2 with engine=claude, model=claude-opus-4-7, effort=high, provider=anthropic, chat-session-id=ecdc00b8-6830-4aa6-8679-f2eab9f154ee. State file flipped to session 2 in-progress.

Implemented deliverable (a) — writer-side totalSessions:null for plan-less Not-Started session sets. Changes: (i) ai_router/start_session.py — added --total-sessions CLI arg; (ii) ai_router/session_state.py — register_session_start's resolution chain reduced to caller -> existing state -> spec.md config block -> spec.md ### Session N headings -> null; the pre-Set-046 max(spec_titles, completed, session_number) fallback removed because the session_number branch was the bug; plan-less branch emits state with no sessions[] key and totalSessions:null; refusal with SessionStateInvariantError on incoherent input (closed sessions but no resolvable total).

Mirror change on the read side so plan-less state round-trips correctly: ai_router/progress.py + tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/src/utils/progress.ts both drop legacy_current/legacyCurrent from the v2->v3 synthesizer's total-candidate set. Without this change, a plan-less in-progress state would synthesize as total=1 from currentSession=1 alone, defeating the writer's null intent. The Explorer's existing fractionFor() carve-out renders the resulting totalSessions:null as 0/?.

Added 6 new tests: 5 in ai_router/tests/test_start_session.py (plan-less session 1 writes null, --total-sessions CLI arg locks count, plan-less refuses session_number > 1, writer refuses state with prior completed when total unresolvable, plan-less state round-trips through read_progress with rule 1) plus 1 in ai_router/tests/test_progress.py (synthesizer does not inflate total from currentSession). Updated existing test fixture _fresh_set to use the canonical ## Session Set Configuration heading (the fixture had been silently relying on the removed session_number fallback).

Mid-session operator incident: a Codex hook on a second machine triggered the Set 033 H3 + Set 036 H4 poll/force/dismiss toast against the in-progress 046 set just as staff was about to be onboarded. Operator directed: keep checkin/checkout mechanics, disable enforcement. Out-of-scope for original Set 046 plan, shipped this session in response. Changes: (i) ai_router/start_session.py adds _coordination_enforced() predicate gating the H3 refusal AND the chatSessionId-mismatch TTY prompt — default off, opt back in via DABBLER_ENFORCE_CHECKOUT_COORDINATION=1; (ii) tools/dabbler-ai-orchestration/scripts/claude-session-start-invoker.js conflict-record emission gated on the same env var (belt and suspenders); (iii) ai_router/tests refusal-path tests now set the env var via monkeypatch so coverage stays intact; (iv) new test test_different_holder_default_off_writes_through_without_refusal locks the default-off production behavior; (v) CLAUDE.md documents the flip and how to re-enable.

Operator directed scope reduction in light of the incident: only deliverable (a) ships in Set 046. Deliverables (b), (c) and the four follow-on enrichments (live cost, idle time, state-divergence pill, needs-migration expansion) are CANCELLED — future work in this area routes through Set 048+ via feedback_audit_then_spec_for_substantial_features. Rewrote spec.md to a 2-session arc, updated session-state.json to a 2-session plan with claude+anthropic as holder (reclaiming the slot from codex+openai that wrote in after the enforcement disable per default-off semantics). The audit artifacts in docs/proposals/2026-05-26-explorer-enrichment-from-harvest-records/ are preserved for any future revival.

Wrote new memory project_set_033_enforcement_disabled with the flip + how-to-re-enable + open follow-up (extension's CheckoutPollService and chatSessionMismatchModal not yet deleted — deferred to Set 047 per the operator's redesign). Authored change-log.md for Set 046 covering Session 1 + Session 2 + the cancelled scope.

Full Python test suite passes: 818 passed, 1 skipped in ~157s. No release this set — Set 046's writer change rides Set 047's release. Session 2 routed cost: $0 (self-review only, no cross-provider verification given the reduced scope).
